title: QME Validation Protocol pre-registration
description: Machine-readable pre-registration for a QME validation study. A conforming document fixes the claim, the primary metric, the decision thresholds, the exclusion policy, and the negative assertions BEFORE any result exists. The spec is protocol/QME_VALIDATION_PROTOCOL_v1.0.md; where this schema and the spec disagree, the spec wins and the schema has a bug.

protocol_version.description: Version of the QME Validation Protocol this pre-registration conforms to.

prereg_id.description: Stable slug naming this study, e.g. 'd1_offset_audit_2026-06-10'.

registered_utc.description: Date the pre-registration was committed, before any result existed.

registration_evidence.description: Proof that registration preceded results. The canonical registration is the committed document; this JSON is its machine-readable form.

canonical_document.description: Repo-relative path of the human-readable pre-registration of record.

git_commit.description: Commit that introduced the canonical document.

retrofit_note.description: Required when this JSON was transcribed from the canonical document after registration. States the transcription date and that the canonical document remains authoritative.

statement.description: The single falsifiable claim under test, stated so that FAIL is a publishable outcome.

claimant.description: 'self' for QME auditing its own stack; 'external' for a third-party claim submitted for validation.

definition.description: Exact estimator, including what is averaged over and any correction applied.

decision_rules.description: The complete pre-committed mapping from metric outcomes to verdicts. Must include at least one PASS rule and one FAIL rule. GRAY_ZONE rules must state how the zone is handled (operator decision, documented at decision time).

condition.description: Human-readable statement of the rule, e.g. 'sd(delta) < 0.15 V across offset points'.

exclusion_policy.description: How datapoints may be dropped. Exclusions are legitimate ONLY through rules written here before results existed, every exclusion is logged, and every exclusion triggers a sensitivity analysis showing the verdict with and without the excluded point.

negative_assertions.description: Claims that must NOT be made, regardless of outcome. Each entry names the forbidden claim and, where one exists, the mechanical guard that enforces it.

guard.description: Script or check that mechanically enforces the assertion, e.g. 'scripts/consistency_check.py'.

anchors.description: Experimental reference points the computation is judged against. Sources are experiment-anchored literature values, never computed references.

tier.description: Anchor confidence: A = clean plateau, B = usable with caveats, C = flagged.

method.description: Everything fixed about how the numbers are produced. Enough detail that a deviation is detectable.

source_policy.description: Spin manifolds come from literature physics (established oxidation states, spin states, and computational conventions), never tuned to improve agreement.

datapoints.description: The units of evidence the verdict is computed over, with their current status. Status moves forward only (pending -> banked -> included or excluded_*); an excluded point keeps its exclusion rule reference.

exclusion_rule.description: Required when status is excluded_*: the exclusion_policy rule invoked.

post_registration_addenda.description: Everything that happened AFTER registration, kept separate from the registered core so the two cannot be confused. Deviations are disclosed here, never silently absorbed.

verdict_invariance.description: Evidence that the verdict does not depend on the deviation, when demonstrable.

verdict_artifact.description: Repo-relative path of the structured verdict JSON this study produces.

generator.description: Script that regenerates the verdict artifact from committed inputs.

provenance_manifest.description: Path of the provenance manifest (file hashes, environment, prereg reference) once generated.

operator_gates.description: Human approval points. At minimum: no compute before slate approval, no ledger write before verdict review.

verdict_state.description: PASS and FAIL per the pre-committed decision rules; GRAY_ZONE requires the pre-registered handling (operator decision, documented); excluded_reproduction_inconclusive is the verdict when reproduction of the underlying result is inconclusive and no PASS or FAIL can honestly be issued.

datapoint_status.description: pending = not yet computed; banked = result exists but no verdict computed yet; included = counted in the verdict statistic; excluded_* = dropped under a pre-committed exclusion rule (logged, sensitivity analysis mandatory).
